fields.narrative_intro: *An overdressed person and an unbothered person discuss ChatGPT 5.5 Pro solving open math research in a server room during an outage.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
OVERDRESSED PERSON: "Gowers gave it Nathanson's Sumset Problem."
UNBOTHERED PERSON: "The unsolved one? Cute."
fields.panel2:
[build]
OVERDRESSED PERSON: "It hit a quadratic upper bound for h=2."
OVERDRESSED PERSON: "In seventeen minutes."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
UNBOTHERED PERSON: "How long for a PhD mathematician?"
OVERDRESSED PERSON: "Gowers said... a week or two."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The unbothered person's coffee mug slowly tips sideways, spilling in slow motion. Neither character moves. Emergency backup lights flicker on, casting red across rows of silent servers.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
OVERDRESSED PERSON: "Then it improved exponential-in-k to O(k^(10h³))."
UNBOTHERED PERSON: "...in the same hour?"
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A framed PhD diploma on the server room wall slides quietly off its nail and lands face-down on the floor. Neither character looks at it.]

narrative_summary: Two characters process ChatGPT 5.5 Pro solving PhD-level open math in under an hour, culminating in a PhD diploma silently falling off the wall.
rationale: Candidate 5 earns its punchline through pure visual restraint — the PhD diploma sliding off the wall while neither character reacts is a genuinely fresh sight gag that doesn't editorialize, letting the specific numbers (17 minutes, a week of PhD work, O(k^(10h³))) do the devastating work. The 'neither character looks at it' button is the best closing beat in the set because it adds a layer of denial that the other candidates' whiteboard gags can't match.
story_title: A recent experience with ChatGPT 5.5 Pro
place: in a server room during an outage
hat_pair: orange traffic cone worn as a hat
